title: PayPal Developer Dashboard CDP Login and App Creation
desc: CDP automation for developer.paypal.com. Key lessons: login field interaction rules, Live/Sandbox switching, Create App with Transaction Search permission for paypal_license.py.
steps: NEVER touch password/email fields via CDP -- clears browser autofill silently, login fails
NEVER use title-based grap on Korean PayPal page -- encoding corruption in grap pattern
Best login: wkappbot a11y invoke '{hwnd:CHROME_HWND,domain:www.paypal.com}#But_btnLogin' when autofill already filled
Create App: Merchant type, enable Transaction Search permission (required for Transactions API)
Store as GitHub Secrets: PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID + PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET on wkappbot-sdk repo

sandbox vs live: URL shows /sandbox even after clicking Live -- use direct navigation location.href='https://developer.paypal.com/dashboard/applications/live' to force switch|Live credentials require PayPal Business account -- 'Upgrade your account to PayPal for Business to view live credentials' shown in yellow banner if still on personal account

sandbox vs live: URL shows /sandbox even after clicking Live -- use direct navigation location.href='https://developer.paypal.com/dashboard/applications/live' to force switch|Live credentials require PayPal Business account -- 'Upgrade your account to PayPal for Business to view live credentials' shown in yellow banner if still on personal account

Private repo NOT needed for license system -- collaborator goes to kiexpert/wkappbot-sdk (matches LicenseManager.cs). PayPal state.json stored in wkappbot-sdk/.github/paypal_state.json. Audit trail = GitHub invite history + Slack notifications, no metadata files needed.

Private repo NOT needed for license system -- collaborator goes to kiexpert/wkappbot-sdk (matches LicenseManager.cs). PayPal state.json stored in wkappbot-sdk/.github/paypal_state.json. Audit trail = GitHub invite history + Slack notifications, no metadata files needed.

Cookie consent popup + 2FA Next button: both block CDP synthetic events (dispatchEvent, .click(), PostMessage). 2FA page uses React synthetic events. Only physical mouse click works. Workaround: user must manually click Next + enter OTP. Then take over after login for credential extraction. Document this so automation starts AFTER 2FA completion.

Cookie consent popup: blocks UIA find (no buttons visible) and synthetic dispatchEvent. Only physical click works -- move window on-screen first: wkappbot win-move then physical mouse click at (X=558, Y=468) for accept button. PostMessage and CDP dispatchEvent both blocked by PayPal popup.
author: claude
